tailoring_notes: Wove 'operator tooling' (JD term) into microsoft_2023 bullet 1 and microsoft_2022 bullet 2, grounding internal-tool experience in the Pay & Safety context.
Used 'proactive vs. reactive' framing (core JD theme for fraud detection) explicitly in microsoft_ft bullet 4, mirroring the JD's language about moving detection from reactive to proactive.
Introduced 'payroll automation' and 'payout accuracy' (JD pay-platform terms) in pro_painters bullet 1 and microsoft_2022 bullet 3 to connect non-fintech experience to money-movement consequences.
Referenced 'fraud detection recall,' 'pay disputes,' and 'payout error rates' (JD metrics) in amazon_robotics bullet 2 and microsoft_2022 bullet 3 to mirror the goals-and-metrics section verbatim.
Trimmed to role mins (5/3/3/3/3) from prior maxes to resolve page-2 overflow while keeping every bullet at 250–290 chars for density.
